//: Project-wide VS Code settings — make TS editor experience match CLI
//tsdk-note: VS Code defaults to its bundled TS version which can differ from the project's. Pointing tsdk at apps/web/node_modules/typescript means the TS service uses the SAME version `npx tsc --noEmit` does — so editor squiggles match CLI errors.
//apps-mobile-note: When you open a file under apps/mobile/, the TS Language Server walks up from the file to find the nearest tsconfig.json (apps/mobile/tsconfig.json) and uses ITS compilerOptions. Same for apps/web/ and packages/shared/. No per-folder config needed.
//eslint-note: ESLint extension needs to know each app/package owns its own eslint config (or lack thereof). Without changeProcessCWD it would try to apply web's eslint.config.mjs to mobile files (impossible — mobile has no eslint config and uses different RN-flavored rules).
